meaning: adj.暗中的,秘密的,偷偷摸摸的
examples: She sneaked a surreptitious glance at her watch.
He made a surreptitious entrance to the club through the little door in the brick wall.
Rudolph looked surreptitious ly at his watch.
As cellphones become smarter, they may also offer kids a surreptitious avenue to the Web.
Still, these surreptitious ways of masking tax increases are troublesome from a tax planning perspective.
